title: Java Array Init
description: Practice array literals, indexing, and length.

completionChecks.prompt: What does totalItems hold after this snippet runs?
choices: Compilation fails because the arrays have different lengths
completionChecks.explanation: scores has 3 elements and names has 2, so scores.length + names.length is 5; each array tracks its own length.

completionChecks.prompt: What happens if the code reads scores[3]?
choices: It returns 0 because arrays are zero-filled past the end
Compilation fails because the index is out of range
completionChecks.explanation: scores has valid indexes 0 through 2, so reading scores[3] throws ArrayIndexOutOfBoundsException at runtime; javac does not reject an out-of-range index here.

completionChecks.prompt: What does first hold after int first = scores[0]; runs?
choices: Compilation fails because array literals always need new int[]
completionChecks.explanation: Array indexes start at zero, so scores[0] reads the first element from the initializer, which is 84.
